text.title: the perfect switzerland trip *hikes, paddleboard, paraglide, and travel chaos
text.u: Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
That feels really good.
We made it to Switzerland and we did get
off our flight, pick up a rental car and
then immediately go to the first hike
that we wanted to.
>> So, did we sleep on the flight?
>> My watch says I got 3 hours of sleep.
So, we are doing the Stew's Ridge Trail
hike and we took the steepest finicular
get up here. It was very cool.
>> Are they only called that or is that
Italian? I don't know. I'm gonna say
that we hitched a ride, a very steep
ride to get up here. And then I think
we're going to take a chair lift to get
up even higher before we do a two-mile
hike.
You're about to get sweeped off your
feet.
[Music]
Why do they look bigger than American
cows?
>> New York City.
I cannot believe there is blue sky. It's
so pretty.
>> Wow.
>> So,
why is he up here?
>> Very sweet. And then sour.
[Music]
So,
>> so we
>> are we here or here? I think we came up
this one
>> here.
>> So, there is a chair lift the cling
stock to go from frumple
frmpletock to cling stock.
>> Simple.
Whoa.
>> Oh gosh.
>> I'm so scared that you're going to step
in.
>> Oh no. I don't want you in the car if
you step in one of these boot piles.
God.
[Music]
Very
[Music]
good.
[Music]
[Music]
I was in charge of picking the hotel for
this trip and I feel like I did a really
good job immediately upon checking in.
And it has so much cute character. But
the best part of all is right here.
The view.
I think it's going to be even more
insane when the sun comes out and when
the sky is blue. But the room itself is
very cute. We have a porch. Picture Josh
and I sitting there. A cup of coffee.
And then inside it is very cozy. I feel
like the more that I've booked places to
stay, the more that I want places that
have character. And I'm kind of digging
the green carpet. All of the little wood
features. The bed looks extremely comfy.
And I'm seeing that they did the two
comforter thing, which I feel like is
just a cool touch of giving people their
own comforters. I'll probably end up
stealing both. And a desk area if Josh
needed to edit, but he doesn't because
he's not editing this trip, which is
fun.
Sometimes my issue with places with
character is that they feel very old or
like they could be dirty. This bathroom
feels exceptionally clean. So, I'm
loving that. And I'm already scheduling
when I need to have a bath because that
looks so nice. I just realized this is a
towel warmer.
Day is made. Slippers, robes. Okay,
every door that I open, I'm loving this
place more and more. This will be me
tomorrow morning.
transition to me with a coffee tomorrow.
>> Josh and I just got back from dinner. We
are so tired. We showered, kind of got
our stuff like put away and picked out
for tomorrow morning because tomorrow is
supposed to be the one day that is like
completely full sun. So, we want to go
on another hike that we really have on
our mind, especially since the one today
was like so cloudy. And I feel like
that's such a pivotal thing when you're
going to obviously viewpoints is that
you're in the mountains, so there's
clouds most of the time. So, if there's
a full Sunday, we wanted to take
advantage of that. So, I'm pretty sure
we're waking up at like 4:30 in the
morning, which is not going to be
pleased. Like, which wow, I can't even
speak right now. We are waking up at
4:30 in the morning. I will not be
pleased in the morning. I also don't
know where I'm going to get coffee or
breakfast or food. And as a planner, I
don't like that. But I know that we're
going to have a really good day. And
even just being here, driving in already
doing a hike, seeing the town we're
staying in, I'm like blown away how
magical it is. So, I know that tomorrow
is going to be just the same, if not
even better. There's supposed to be a
lake at the bottom that we can swim in.
So, that's going to be really exciting,
too. But all of that to say, we are
exhausted and I need to go to sleep
because I'm going to be up in far too
little hours.
Well, we definitely have a situation. We
parked our car in the parking garage
because Wen, the town we're staying in,
is a carless village. So, we're here
parked down in Lauder Brunen and we have
our parking ticket in our hotel room.
So, we literally woke up at 4:30 a.m.
Got down to the train station, took the
train, came down here, whatever. And
then the next train to go back up isn't
until 6:00 a.m. But we have like walked
around the parking garage, tried to call
the contact office, tried to pay it
online, tried to find anybody working
here. No one is working yet. So the next
train to go back up was 6:00 a.m. So
we've waited in the car. Josh is now on
that train, but he has 10 minutes to run
to the hotel, find the parking ticket,
run back to the train station before
that train leaves. And if he misses that
one, it's another half an hour. So, it
is really sad because we woke up like
hoping to see sunrise going on all of
this and then just like a silly mistake.
Makes it where, you know, we can't do
that. But Josh feels really bad that he
forgot. I feel bad because I moved the
parking ticket. I put it in like another
bag of stuff cuz I was like, "Oh, let me
just pick up." And so I'm like, "You
don't have to feel bad. There's
literally I don't know. That's on both
of us. I should have thought of it,
too." But yeah, it is so weird that
there's not like something we can do to
just pay a maximum fee or anything.
You're just stuck. And with the train
station schedules and all of that. So
yeah, definitely took a turn for making
this morning later. I'm tracking Josh's
dot and he is running so fast. I
literally feel like I'm watching a
little video game. And he made his way
to the hotel, assuming he found it
because now he's booking his way back.
So fingers crossed that he makes that
train coming back down. I feel so
useless like sitting here. And I hope I
have water for him.
Wow, you did run
up and back and get the ticket in 7
minutes.
>> Yes.
>> I didn't think it was even going to be
possible.
>> You were running so fast.
>> Yeah.
Well, I always say when you have a
little hiccup for something, you were
protected for something or we don't know
what. So, we're having a slower start to
our morning, but we do have water. And
we
>> had a slow start.
>> I had the most aggressive start.
>> We had a little delayed start to our
morning, but we can finally get out of
the parking garage.
>> Wait, let's have a real time emotional
breakdown right now. I'm going to sob.
>> Oh my gosh,
>> all of that just
>> get out of here.
>> Get out of the parking garage. Um, now
we're taking our trek up to a very
beautiful hike and it looks like a
gorgeous day. So, we will not let this
get us down.
[Music]
You having a nice snap?
[Music]
>> Hey buddy,
me and my fam
Switzerland.
shedding multiple layers because we are
about to be going up.
>> Well, first looks.
>> I'm so excited. I think I'm going to
swim. That was like one of the big
points of this hike was that you can
swim at the bottom. But when I started
it in my puffy like fuzzy sherpa, that
was Josh's. I was like, there's
absolutely no way. Wait, Josh, look how
where we came from.
zoom. That's crazy.
And we're a mile in the third in
[Music]
[Music]
That hurts.
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
But it's like I don't want Drop it down.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
They were so nervous.
[Music]
[Music]
Wow, that is cool.
I'm going.
>> Wow.
I'm tired.
She currently at 12 hours awake steps.
Don't need to gas me up like that.
What's going on?
>> I'm very tired.
>> I need raw time. You're not scheduling
in any raw time.
>> What did we do that was so demanding
today?
>> 30k steps.
Why are you not needing any rest? I need
food.
>> In the past two nights, we've gotten a
collective 8 hours of sleep.
>> Which is fine. I'm very tired.
>> Mhm.
>> So, you're just going to sleep while the
sky is blue?
>> No. I You're Do you not understand
rotting? It's laying for some time.
>> So, you're just going to lay inside when
the sky is blue?
>> Yes, I have to.
We spent some time rotting and I was
looking through the photos of today. I'm
already so excited. Everything was
absolutely gorgeous. And I did have a
little guilt of the sun being out and
the sky being so pretty and just
chilling. But especially since being up
so early and then having like a very
intense day, I just needed some time to
chill. So, I did throw on this little
outfit. I'll give you more of a fit
check with both Josh and my fit. But I
think we're gonna eat dinner at our
hotel because we heard that the food is
really, really yummy here and we wanted
to check that out while we're staying
here. Um, so I think we're gonna do
that. And we have
like 2 and 1/ half hours until sunset.
So hopefully dinner won't be too long
and then we can find some little sunset
location to watch that from either our
hotel somewhere around in town. But it
feels really, really good to have a
little bit of rest and to have had such
an amazing day. I can feel myself being
very tired. You guys can probably see
it. I don't know if you can fully see
that, but this is my fit. Just a long
sleeve. The vest is from Midnight Lunch.
And then these are some Old Navy jeans,
the only jeans I packed. And my Adidas
shoes. I fear that I packed really
wrong. I've been cold pretty much this
whole trip. So, I have a lot of shorts
and active sets that I don't know if
I'll be wearing as well as like tank
tops, t-shirts. I brought maxi skirts
with tank tops and I'm like, girl, I'm
cold.
>> Do you think you could do it?
>> Actually, don't know. I feel like I'm
not very good at these surprisingly.
>> If you don't get across, we have to do
the via.
>> You have You have to get across. This is
all that Josh and I can think about
tonight is if we should do the scariest
thing I've ever seen. Quite truly.
>> I cannot do this.
>> Sorry to have no faith in myself, but
like
[Music]
>> Wow.
[Music]
Your ankles are going to snap off.
>> That was good.
>> I feel pretty solid. My weight just
likes to
>> The middle of it is harder.
[Music]
>> That's pretty brutal. We stopped at the
cocktail bar and Josh got this delicious
looking hot chocolate.
>> Very good.
Check this out.
>> Okay.
Not another hike, please.
In an interesting turn of events, we are
going paragliding.
>> Yes.
>> Yes, we are.
>> We just kept seeing the people doing
paragliding here. And then I asked on my
Instagram story and almost every other
one was people saying that you should go
paragliding.
>> So, I feel like at first I was like, is
this just a very touristy thing? And
maybe it is, but we are tourists. I
don't know many tourists that are like,
I'm going to 100% go 3,000 meters high
for my excursion.
>> So, you speak in meters now.
He goes to Switzerland one time. I
>> actually don't know if that's even
accurate
>> because we don't know me.
>> Look up the conversion of 3,000 meters.
>> Um, whoever
>> whoever our editor is,
>> whoever I was just gonna say the same
thing. The longer we're married, the
more we're actually the same person. But
yeah, to whoever's editing this video,
if you want to convert meters to feet,
>> yards,
>> meters,
>> and inches.
>> If you want to convert meters to
centimeters, this is how high we're
going to be going.
Pause for dramatic effect.
Um, I'm not that nervous. I feel like we
kind of need to go into the lore of what
we've been so nervous about. And I'm
just going to put a photo or a video.
just speak its name.
>> The via
My palms are sweating.
>> Every single time we say it,
>> we
>> I feel like I could throw up.
>> It's a hike that you go and there's
metal rungs and you're looking like
2,000 ft down. You have cables clipped
in.
>> How many meters?
>> Please stop. You have cables clipped in.
So, in doing research, as long as you
have the proper equipment, it's beginner
friendly, which is what keeps throwing
us off. Like, why am I reading something
that is beginner friendly and my stomach
feels like it is dropping out of my body
>> to do it? Maybe America has kind of
ruined us because it's like that simply
would not exist.
>> It just just wouldn't. And obviously
something that scary, you need to be
safe. Take all the proper precautions.
But it's like you have the helmet, the
gloves, the cables, the harness, so you
should be safe doing it. And that's what
I feel like we keep getting stuck on.
But then every time we watch photos or
videos of it, we're like, how? So I
think we just had to do something also
like kind of dangerous to make ourselves
feel like to make ourselves feel
something.
>> We came to Switzerland wanting to feel
something.
>> Yeah. So we're going paragliding. This
paragliding it.
[Music]
>> Bye. I'll see you at the bottom.
[Music]
There she goes. That's so crazy.
[Music]
That was awesome.
>> Yeah, let's do it.
[Music]
That was insane. I'm so happy that we
decided to do that. Both of our flight
instructors were literally so nice. And
I feel like I was expecting like when
you go on a helicopter and it's loud.
>> It was so peaceful. It was literally the
prettiest view. So peaceful. We did cool
tricks. They to got like cool videos. We
got to like see each other while we were
going. That was awesome.
>> That was incredible.
>> The view couldn't have been better. 10
out of 10 experience.
>> Weather beautiful.
>> Mountains
beautiful.
>> Life beautiful.
[Music]
I feel like the definition of us
traveling is that we're in a rush. And
it's because we both like being as
efficient as possible and literally
capturing every single thing in one trip
and never wasting a good sunny day and
never wasting a sunset. So, we need to
learn how to slow down.
Why is that giving me anxiety?
I'm so exhausted. A half mile in.
It's feeling a lot more like rock
climbing.
He is our leader.
This is like I'm in race to survive and
the other team is catching up.
Go Josh.
This vlog is not building my portfolio
that I'm athletic.
>> But look where we are.
>> ASMR of me heavy breathing.
Heart rate 149.
Not that bad.
>> Lead the way.
>> Okay,
>> I think I see something.
>> Oh
my
goodness.
That's insane.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
Hey,
[Music]
hey, hey.
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
[Music]
Hold on for dear life. So, we can either
continue our journey down more spines
for miles and miles or follow that back
to the I can't even speak. Follow that
back to the car. We're going with option
B.
[Music]
Now that we've made it through Mordor,
which was the rocks things, if you watch
the Lord of the Rings, then you know
what we're talking about. It looks like
we're in a vegetable p.
>> This is very much Lord of the Rings.
>> Motus,
>> are you going to put that in? Is my
editor going to leave that?
>> I don't know.
Total time 3 hours and 50 minutes. Wow,
we did it.
>> We just woke up and it's 10:15. Our
hotel breakfast closes at 10:30, so I
quickly got dressed and we're heading
down there.
[Music]
[Music]
Two people who have had a lot of hiking
and cannot wait for a massage. My feet
are literally crunched. I need them to
to my
After the most glorious and relaxing
day, sleeping in, getting massages,
hanging out at the pool area, eating
lunch out there, reading.
Am I missing anything?
Not wearing hiking shoes. Guess what?
We're back in hiking shoes. Um, where
we're staying up in Wan, they have a
cable car that goes up to the top of the
mountain, and you can do a very simple
hike there. You can do more hikes, but
there is a pretty simple one. So, we
kind of got fitted in some simple hiking
fits. Hopefully, I don't get too cold. I
am a little nervous, but it's been hot
today. Um,
>> it's warm.
>> Yeah. So, we're going to go catch the
cable car and
>> Oh, we are so fine. We're going to go
catch the cable car,
spend maybe an hour or so up there, and
it's supposed to be very pretty. Let's
go.
Who's going to go back for that?
>> It's not my fault.
>> I think what's so cool and Josh and I
were just talking about is that so many
of the hikes here you can reach by doing
gondilas up or trains or something like
that. So, you can make it like a very
short little trip with a gorgeous view
or you can then embark on harder ones if
you want to do more mileage. But, I feel
like there's not a lot of places I've
been to that is accessible for like
wherever you're at in your hiking
journey and that's cool. Update. We
decided to go on a two and a half mile
hike because it's looking very paved and
downward like this.
It says that it's going to take us about
an hour and 10 minutes. Then we're going
to arrive at a town called Kleinsteg, I
think is how you say it. And once we
arrive there, we have 10 minutes to make
the last train out of there. So, I don't
really know why we left ourselves with
that much pressure, but you know, it's
about being efficient and we had a very
slow, relaxing day. So, why not just amp
up the heart rate with a good hike and a
little angst? Music to my ears,
literally.
[Music]
You set me up.
>> Do it harder.
>> No, Josh. This one is stuck.
Slow
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
[Applause]
down.
Why you talk so mean?
Good lord, man. It ain't what you say.
Got nice clothes and late night shows.
Boogie down at the bar
and homemade booze, but it always goes
too far.
>> One of the things I wanted to try in
Switzerland was fresh milk from the side
of the road. But apparently they have
vending machines where you can actually
just dispense the milk yourself and get
it straight from the cow. So had to
check that out.
>> That is it.
>> You open it up. Well, and just like
that, we have a liter of fresh milk.
That was so much fun.
>> Cheers.
Look who's crossing the road.
They're coming for their milk.
No, this is actually unreal.
Did we buy insurance if a cow hits the
car?
This is crazy.
[Music]
I got
[Music]
[Music]
nice.
>> If you as a woman are watching this and
wondering what are they doing, just know
that we did take the time to stop over
at the lake for probably at least 10
minutes to make sure Josh can get the
perfect rock skip. And I said it was
nice and he said that's all. So I am
telling him he should send it in his
voice group message because it's nice.
>> It was an amazing rock skip.
>> It's nice. This is my position right
now. I have it on five times one. So do
the eclipse. Nope. Duck crossing. You
need to wait now.
>> I'll wait.
That was pretty.
That was insane.
Yay! He did it.
I can't believe I get to be married.
>> All right, let me show you how it's
done.
>> Uh,
that's how you I think you have to get
lower.
>> Man, slaining.
Let me show you.
And then take it back and then allow
and then release. Yep. Okay.
The depths are now just waiting here.
So, we're going to have to take an
intermission before I can put that into
work.
>> Or go that way. The video can see
myself there.
I do.
I told you I wasn't going to be good
that way.
>> That was the best noise ever.
>> Really?
>> We checked into our little lakefront
Airbnb that's down in more of the
village area or like the lakefront area.
And it is so cute. Driving in, it was
rainy and cloudy and all of that. And
then I was like, man, I love the rain.
It looks so cute, but also it would be
really cool to see a pretty sunset. We
went to the grocery store, kind of
walked around the town, got a little
settled in, and now look at the clouds.
Josh was just out here admiring the
view.
He looks so cute.
[Music]
[Music]
We're having a really romantic date
night.
>> Yeah.
>> The best table in the house. This quaint
little town.
We just ordered some fish and a corn and
blue. We're going to do a little
splitzies. That's what we love. That's
my love language. And John had his best
rock ski today.
>> Yeah, I did.
That's all I can think about. Like
genuinely all I can think about.
>> Yeah. I mean, that's to be expected.
>> I'm really good at rocking.
>> Possibly the best.
>> And if everyone can see
>> you would say that.
>> Yes.
>> You would say I'm the best rock skipper.
>> Yes.
outside.
Okay.
Nice.
>> Josh caught our walk short again
because the water is more still.
Okay.
>> Did you see that?
>> I did and I got it on video. Good job.
No. Leave on high.
>> I could do better.
>> My little brother asked us a question
and it's always on my mind. And he said,
"Would you rather have a guaranteed 10
more years of your life? You have 10
left. That's it. Or you have a
guaranteed 40." But you always have a
snail that if it touches you, you
instantly die.
>> So,
>> and the snail's mission is to touch you.
>> Yes. But like a snail moves so slow, so
you could always outrun it. You're
always faster, always better. But it's
like you would never rest. So anyway, we
saw this snail in Switzerland and I'm
going to send my brother a photo and be
like, "We found your snail." So you're
good unless the snail finds a way to get
into an airport.
>> He gets on our car. We bring him to the
airport. He gets on the plane, then he's
in the US.
>> He could make his way.
>> He parachutes down out of the plane on
top of Freedom.
>> Yeah.
>> Shout out Freedom.
>> So, we're letting him know that his
snail is in Switzerland. So, pending he
doesn't do that, he's safe for a little
while longer.
>> You stay right there.
Watch this view from our window.
Let's go.
[Music]
I saw you.
[Music]
How could I come to such a pretty place
and not have my favorite coffee ever? We
are attempting by getting a little
portable espresso machine to make our
coffee. And then we have local honey and
the fresh milk we got yesterday. So, I'm
excited for a latte this morning. And
let's make some.
I sound like the cows on the the hikes
we've been going on.
>> Give me a little cow, though.
[Music]
Whoa.
Nice.
>> Okay, after
15 minutes, we got our first sip. Here
we go.
>> It smells really good, but I am a little
scared just seeing how this was pumped
out.
Josh, that is very good. Like I almost
didn't
>> You're looking at me.
>> Well, yeah, cuz I'm talking to you.
>> The camera's all the way up here.
>> Here's the thing. I did not want this to
be so good because that was so much
effort and I kind of wanted it to be
like that effort. It couldn't be. That
hits.
>> You did You did even tell me you're
like, "Oh, no. You can have the first
one."
Yeah, I did.
>> Because you were expecting it to be bad.
>> This is very good.
>> Yeah. Give me my coffee back.
>> I know that I'm biased, but I our beans
are the best. Okay.
>> Best coffee we had in Europe in
Switzerland.
>> I'm not going to say that cuz we've had
really good coffee, but it's my
favorite.
>> Here, I'll turn the camera away.
>> That is the best coffee I've had this
whole trip.
Huh?
We definitely took this morning as a
slow morning. We enjoyed making our
coffee, which was like a whole process.
And now it's lunchtime and we're going
to go start a walk. There's a waterfall
that I guess is I think a 3mile walk
from here, but it's all just paved in a
nice way. So, put on my rain jacket and
some warmer clothes because today is
definitely our cloudiest and rainiest
yet. But we're not letting that keep us
inside all day. So, we're going to do
that and then maybe take a bus or a
ferry back here. Either way, sounds
pretty magical.
>> Your water bottle looks so silly.
>> Does it?
>> No, it doesn't.
>> I said that I would bring the big
backpack because Josh put his rain
jacket in my very small bag. This bag
perfectly fits my water bottle, tripod,
film, camera, phone, wallet.
With this rain jacket, it doesn't fit my
water bottle. And I was like, I'll just
bring your big backpack if you're going
to put stuff in there, too. And he's
like, no, no, this fits perfect. So, no.
>> I think it looks great. It compliments
your outfit.
>> That's what I was asking. I just can't
see
>> the cap. Look cap
lid.
I don't know where the orange fits in,
but the orange is complimentary.
Okay, I'm trusting you that I don't look
like a queen.
[Laughter]
[Applause]
[Music]
[Music]
Josh in his natural habitat. at eating
an apple with a plant stand.
[Music]
[Music]
She's shaking.
>> All right. Press up.
[Music]
>> Yeah.
[Music]
[Laughter]
We may have gotten a to- go carrot cake.
[Music]
[Music]
Oh yeah.
>> Wa.
>> That's a different cure cake.
>> Is it?
>> It's like lemony.
>> Interesting.
>> Taste it.
>> I will.
Oh,
>> isn't it different?
>> It's the icing. The icing has to be like
>> like a lemon,
>> but the cake itself isn't overly sweet.
It's almost like a granola bar.
>> Yeah.
Oh, grocery delivery.
Thank you. Some fresh pastries, yogurt.
The coffee is starting. Fresh cheese
grated. Eggs are starting to cook.
>> And the sun.
>> And the sun is out today.
>> Honey.
[Music]
>> What?
>> No. There's a bee who wants his honey
back. But I just need a little more.
>> He's going to come get you.
>> I know. He's been following me around
the kitchen.
I hear him.
>> He's so
>> Oh, there he is.
>> I'm closing the honey.
>> You're supposed to help me in these
situations.
>> He's just hanging by the window.
>> I know. I want him to be happy in life.
I just want him to go out the window and
not be upset at me.
>> Okay, go out.
>> Close the window. He went out.
>> Good morning. It is our last full day
here and it is so stunning today. I feel
like yesterday with it being cloudy and
gloomy, it was fine because we went on a
waterfall hike and just kind of got to
see everything. It still was very green
and lush, but I was hoping for sun on
the last day. And it hasn't quite hit
the water yet, but we're hoping for
beautiful water, which I don't see why
we want to have. And we actually found a
place when we were on our walk that
rents stand up paddle boards. So, we are
going to go paddle boarding on Lake
Brens.
>> I'm pretty excited.
>> It does sound magical. I did just
realize I forgot my sunglasses. So, if
I'm squinting, that's why.
>> This might be the most perfect rock
ever.
>> You think we're going to end up
swimming?
>> Yes.
[Music]
We made it out onto the lake and it is
so so beautiful. I can't even believe
how blue it is. But Josh had a vision
for the drone. So, he's going to try to
put the drone up on a paddle board in
the middle of this lake. Hopefully, a
ferry doesn't go past. And I guess if
you see the footage,
then you know that we were successful.
And if not,
I'm so sorry to our drone.
[Music]
Heat.
Hey, heat. Hey, heat.
[Music]
I'm finding a lot of peace right now.
This trip is the peace that I needed.
[Music]
>> Huh?
>> It's a perfect
We paddled from the place that we rented
our boards back to our house and now
we're going to drop off the drone and
Josh's jacket because we do not need a
jacket anymore and we're trying to amp
ourselves up to swim. So, we'll see if
that happens. It's so clear and blue
though that I really want to. Hey, why
are you ease dropping?
>> Are my socks in it?
>> Yes.
>> Action socks.
>> Josh always wears compressions because
vein stuff.
So now you know.
I've always been an ocean girl, but also
paddling in the lake here is so
refreshing. There's no salt in the
water. I will say it is very cold. It's
58 degrees outside, but it feels a lot
warmer. Like with the sun on me, I'm
actually cooking. I'm like, should I
have put on some sunscreen? I don't
know. But it's so peaceful, so relaxing.
Josh is on his board in front of me and
we're just taking in the blue water, the
blue skies. It is so clear, so very
clear that I know a dip in would be
good. I just don't know if I'm feeling
brave enough. As much as we've taken a
lot of time on this trip to capture
content, be vlogging, taking photos, all
of that stuff, we've also had a lot of
downtime of just doing enjoying things
together. Josh and my favorite thing to
do is activities. And I feel like hiking
is a really bonding thing because you're
doing something challenging with a
reward together. And like it's very
sweet. He'll offer to help carry my bags
or I will pick up the sandwiches for us
or share water or just do things. I
don't know. like there's something just
very precious about accomplishing things
together and I feel like that's why we
enjoy it so much especially living in
Florida where it is so flat there and I
love Florida. I feel like I I post about
it all the time. I love the beach. I
love everything about it. But getting to
travel places like this is so eye
opening of how you don't have to live
near the beach to enjoy water obviously
or you don't have to um only enjoy the
sunsets there, but it makes you
appreciate even though Florida doesn't
have any mountains or hikes or things
like that how great the weather is or
there's just pros and cons of every
different place. And I feel like I
always say that when I get comments of
people being like, "Oh, I would be happy
or enjoy my life if I lived in Florida
or warm weather." And while I will say
that there is truth behind the sun
bringing a lot of happiness and joy and
so I get that a lot, but there's also so
much joy to be had when it's like, wow,
all of these hikes that you get to do or
all of the greenery that comes with this
or the elevation or how the air is so
crisp or people can work outside because
you're not sweating to death and
sweltering in the summer. So, I feel
like that's an important thing about
life is obviously you have to do what's
best for you and live in the places and
do things that bring you happiness, but
that the grass isn't always greener on
the other side and there's always things
to be thankful for or to just appreciate
about whatever your situation is.
Obviously, easy to say being in
Switzerland, but I remember thinking
that when I visited the Midwest or out
out west anywhere or just all of these
different places have so many cool
things about them. So, I'm just having a
grateful moment right now. Place where
we got our paddle boards does have a
little dock over there. And I think if
I'm going to jump somewhere, it's going
to be there.
>> Three, two, one.
>> Cold. Really cold.
Wow. Like I'm inside of a water bottle
with ice.

Oh gosh.
Oh boy.

we're going to go grab lunch and then go
to this cute valley and stream little

Josh is also trying to pick out his

>> The white skirt. I feel like the white

>> Try that right now.
>> I will.
>> We're currently driving to a very pretty
valley and stream and I feel like I'd
seen this on Tik Tok. It's so funny to
me the amount of places that I've seen.
No one has mentioned the driving to them
and everything is like little teeny
bridges. This is honestly the easiest
part of it, but the road can only fit
one car and it will be sheer cliff
switchbacks. Buses will be going up on
the other end. And so Josh and I were
just laughing. We're like, "How is it
that no one mentions how crazy these
drives are?"
>> And people are saying it's like the one
or two comments that it's a fairy tale
drive or that it's like the most scenic
beautiful drive. And no doubt,
>> no,
>> it's pretty, but if you if you make a
mistake, you actually do die. like
forewarning or fair warning. It's like
>> your hands are probably cramped.
>> It's sheer and there's no protection.
You're exposed.
>> So, I feel like we haven't really rented
a car in other countries before. We
usually do public transportation. We've
been able to do way more things with
having a rental car, but with that comes
its own fair share of challenges. All
right.
